name: Bab al-Mandab (Red Sea)
description: Narrow strait connecting the Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden. ~15 submarine cables transit this chokepoint carrying the majority of Europe-Asia traffic.

name: Strait of Malacca
description: Strait between Malaysia and Indonesia. Major bottleneck for cables connecting Southeast Asia, East Asia, and the Indian Ocean.

name: Baltic Sea
description: Semi-enclosed sea in Northern Europe. Multiple submarine cables connecting Nordic and Baltic states. Recent sabotage incidents (2024).

name: Luzon Strait
description: Strait between Taiwan and the Philippines. Major junction for trans-Pacific cables. Vulnerable to earthquakes and submarine landslides.

name: Guam
description: Pacific island hub where many trans-Pacific cables converge. Single-point-of-failure risk for Pacific connectivity.

name: English Channel
description: Narrow strait between England and France. Critical junction for transatlantic cables connecting to Europe and UK-continent links.
